name: Vegan Omelette with Boiled Plantain
intro: Plantain for breakfast is very common in Nigeria and if it isn’t served with a stew, it is served with eggs. Here we will make a bean-based vegan omelette that’s bursting with fresh and flavourful vegetables.

ingredients.item: ripe yellow plantains 1/2 cup beans flour 1/2 cup water
1/2 teaspoon salt (or black salt/kala namak for an eggy flavour) Sunflower oil for frying

method: Cut the tops and tails of the plantains and use a knife to score the skin all the way down the side. Leave the skin on and cut each plantain into 3-4 large chunks. Place them in a pot and cover completely with water. Bring to a boil then cook on medium heat for 20 minutes until tender. Drain the excess water, leave the plantains to cool for a few minutes then peel and discard the skins.
To make the vegan omelette, combine the beans flour, water, chopped onions, peppers, chilli, sweetcorn and salt in a bowl. Mix well and set aside for 10 minutes.

Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a small non-stick frying pan and pour in half of the omelette batter. Gently spread it around the bottom of the pan with the back of a spoon to form a round omelette, cover and cook for 5 minutes over medium-high heat. Use a spatula to loosen the sides and gently flip the omelette over to cook on the other side for a further 2-3 minutes. Gently fold the omelette in half and transfer to a plate. Repeat this process with the rest of the batter to make another omelette. The omelette will appear stiﬀ at first but will soften as it starts to cool.
